communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Records Retention
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Current records retention schedules and list of records retained by your office. 
- FOIA/public records logs from 2020 to today's date. 
Please release in CSV/Excel format, but we will accept PDF, whichever is their original file type.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Detroit tax formula
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documentation sufficient to explain the City of Detroit’s residential valuation methodology for the 2024 and 2025 assessment rolls, including:

- Economic Condition Factor (ECF) studies and calculations
- Residential land value tables
- Depreciation schedules
- Cost tables used in residential valuation
- CAMA valuation methodology documentation
- Assessment model specifications or calibration materials
- Internal guidance or manuals used by appraisal staff
- Neighborhood stratification or ECF boundary documentation

I am also requesting any documentation describing how assessed values are derived from property characteristics and market data within the city’s residential appraisal system.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Monroe County State Attorney "Brady List"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

CJ Ciaramella

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oklahoma Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear Oklahoma Governor’s Office records official,
I hope this request finds you well. Pursuant to the Oklahoma Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records from Dec. 1 2025 to the date this request is processed.

All email communications, including attachments, between Oklahoma Governor Kevin Stitt, anyone communicating on the governor’s behalf such as an Executive Assistant, Scheduler or Receptionist/Secretary, Chief of Staff, Chief Deputy, Solicitor General, Communications Director, Press Secretary and any of the individuals or domains listed below: 

"@alec.org"
"@allianceforconsumers.org"
"@americanenergyinstitute.com"
"@api.org"
"@boydengray.com"
"@boydengrayassociates.com"
"@consumersdefense.org"
"@consumersresearch.org"
"@crcadvisors.com"
"@crcpublicrelations.com"
"@crcstrategies.com"
"@holtzmanvogel.com"
"@lifepowered.org"
"@texaspolicy.com"
"@varidonstrategies.com"
"@who.eop.gov"
“@kochinc.com”
“@kochps.com” 
“@kochstrategies.com”
“@kochind.com”
“@kochcompanies.com”
“@kcps.com”
“@sfof.com"
“julie.daniels@oksenate.gov”
“anthony.moore@okhouse.gov”


All email communications, including attachments, between Oklahoma Governor Kevin Stitt, anyone communicating on the governor’s behalf such as an Executive Assistant, Scheduler or Receptionist/Secretary, Chief of Staff, Chief Deputy, Solicitor General, Communications Director, Press Secretary and the below list of domains… 

	“@oak.ok.gov”
	“@usdoj.gov” 
“@oksenate.gov” 

	That include any of the following keywords…


	“SB 1439”
	“Energy Freedom Act”
	“Criminal profiteering”
	“Public nuisance”
	“Energy security”
	“Energy freedom”
	“Liability waiver”
	“Liability shield”
	“Fossil fuels”
	“Greenhouse gases” 
	“lawfare”
	“Climate change”
	
I am making this request on behalf of Fieldnotes, a news media organization, and I plan to make the requested documents available to the general public. This request is not for commercial purposes. If you anticipate that there will be fees, I would be grateful if you could provide an estimate of the total charges before fulfilling my request.

In the event you withhold portions of the requested records as exempt from disclosure, please detail which exemption you believe justifies each withholding and disclose any reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of the records. If a record is withheld in its entirety, please explain why it is unreasonable to separate out portions of the record for release.

Thank you in advance for your assistance. I look forward to receiving your prompt response to this request, as statute requires. If you have any questions or suggestions about this request please don't hesitate to reach out. 

Best regards,
Ari

--

Ariella Plachta
Researcher | Fieldnotes
(747) 254 5758
ariella@fieldnotes.co

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Ariella Plachta

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Hollis
